questions.question: What is the canonical participant × event × instrument long-format schema, and how should it normalize against REDCap exports?
questions.summary: Lock down the tidy long-format schema (one row per participant × event × instrument) as the analysis-ready source of truth. REDCap flattens longitudinal events inconsistently; a stable schema prevents silent joins from drifting across the pipeline.
questions.implementation: Define a normalized table `redcap_long` with keys (subject_surrogate, event_name, instrument, field, value). Wide-format feature matrices are derived from this through documented pivots in build_dashboard_data.py.

questions.question: How are the three cohort groups (ASIB, PT, TD) encoded, and what controlled vocabulary covers all longitudinal events (NICU, 1/2/3/6/9/12/24/36 months CGA)?
questions.summary: Fix a vocabulary for group codes and event names so every downstream asset (mixed models, NDA submission, dashboard filters) uses the same strings. Enforce this via a validator in the context skill.
questions.implementation: `entities.md` defines group codes; `dashboard_schema.md` fixes the event string order. The extractor validator fails the build if new strings appear without documentation.

questions.question: Should the analyst-facing feature matrix live in wide (subject × timepoint × feature) or long (tidy) format, and which is optimal for mixed-effects vs. ML downstream?
questions.summary: Mixed-effects (lme4/nlme) consumes long format; tabular ML consumes wide. Decide which canonical form persists to disk and which is a derived view.
questions.implementation: Persist long-format parquet as ground truth; generate wide views via pandas pivot and R tidyr::pivot_wider at pipeline time. Document the contract in dashboard_schema.md.

questions.question: Which derived variables (HRV indices, PRS, HDA phases) belong in the raw features table vs. a higher-level feature matrix layer?
questions.summary: Separate raw-adjacent derivations (per-segment RMSSD) from analysis-ready features (per-visit summaries). Two layers avoid re-running heavy preprocessing each time a modeler iterates.
questions.implementation: Layer A = segment-level HRV parquet. Layer B = visit-level feature matrix (aggregated, z-scored). Caching keyed by commit SHA + config hash.

questions.question: What is the PHI surface area in the raw dataset, and which columns require hashing or surrogate encoding before any analysis?
questions.summary: Every column flagged `phi_flag=true` in the data dictionary gets dropped before aggregation; subject IDs are hashed with `NANO_ID_SALT` into NANO-#### surrogates.
questions.implementation: `drop_phi` helper in build_dashboard_data.py reads data-dictionary flags; surrogate IDs computed with salted SHA-256.

questions.question: How do we harmonize the 3-month Bayley-4 with legacy Bayley-3 scores collected at earlier timepoints?
questions.summary: Bayley versions differ in normative sample and subtest weights. Use published equating tables or linear regression calibration on the 3-month overlap sample.
questions.implementation: Write a harmonization module that applies edition-specific crosswalks and emits an edition flag per row. Test against the developer-provided equating tables.

questions.question: What is the handling rule for assessors scoring an item at modified (chronological) vs. corrected (post-menstrual) age?
questions.summary: Corrected age is preferred through 24 months for VPT infants. Every assessment row needs a `used_age` field with audit rationale.
questions.implementation: Add `used_age` and `age_days` to REDCap; enforce at the scoring form. Pipeline rejects rows missing this pair.

questions.question: How are ADOS, M-CHAT-R, and SCQ scores reconciled across age-appropriate instruments over the 36-month window?
questions.summary: Each instrument applies to a different age band. Build a composite ASD-risk index using the best instrument available per visit, with an explicit hierarchy and source field.
questions.implementation: Python class `AsdRiskComposite` selects per-visit instrument, applies calibrated severity scores (ADOS CSS), and logs source.

questions.question: What algorithm operationalizes ‘clinically significant developmental concern’ from composite scores?
questions.summary: Pre-register a decision rule (e.g., ≥1 SD below norm on any Bayley domain OR ADOS CSS ≥ 4) before outcome adjudication begins.
questions.implementation: Rule lives in `src/features/outcomes.py` as pure function; versioned; manuscript points at the commit SHA.

questions.question: How should we document inter-rater reliability (IRR) for each instrument and integrate ICC into the dashboard?
questions.summary: Quarterly double-coding on ~10% of assessments. Intraclass correlation thresholds: ICC ≥ 0.75 for continuous, κ ≥ 0.6 for categorical.
questions.implementation: Add `irr_batch_id` to REDCap; compute ICC/κ in R; surface in Data Quality section with instrument chips.

questions.question: What is the MCAR/MAR/MNAR assumption per instrument, and how do we test it empirically?
questions.summary: Run Little’s MCAR test + logistic regression of missingness on observed covariates. Assume MAR conditional on documented auxiliaries; note MNAR risk explicitly.
questions.implementation: R script `tests/missingness_mcar.R` runs Little’s test; Python counterpart in `src/qc/missingness_checks.py`. Report fed into the dashboard quality panel.

questions.question: How many MICE iterations (m) are sufficient for longitudinal HRV + Bayley? Target m=20 — how many within-imputation iterations?
questions.summary: m=20 imputations × 30 within-imputation iterations is the pre-registered default; validated by trace-plot stability and effective-degrees-of-freedom sensitivity.
questions.implementation: mice (R) + scikit-learn IterativeImputer (Python) both configured with m=20; pooling done via Rubin’s rules in a shared helper.

questions.question: What auxiliary variables should enter the imputation model to improve MAR plausibility?
questions.summary: Include SES, maternal depression (EPDS), caregiver work schedule, NICU length-of-stay, and prior-visit HRV. These predict missingness without being targets of analysis.
questions.implementation: Config-driven auxiliary list in `config/imputation_auxiliaries.yml`; unit-tested against MAR simulation.

questions.question: How do we pool estimates across imputations for mixed-effects and latent growth models (Rubin’s rules)?
questions.summary: Use `mice::pool` in R and `statsmodels.miceData` / manual Rubin pooling in Python. For lavaan LGCM, prefer FIML when possible, else pool over imputed datasets with `semTools::runMI`.
questions.implementation: Central helper `pool_estimates(fit_list)` returns pooled coefs, SEs, and df. Used by every inferential report.

questions.question: When should we prefer FIML (lavaan) over multiple imputation for structural models?
questions.summary: FIML when missingness is plausibly MAR conditional on model covariates and sample is large enough; MI when auxiliaries not in the analysis model are needed.
questions.implementation: Decision tree documented in `research_questions.md §Missing Data`. Each published table reports which method was used.

questions.question: What is the primary prediction target — binary autism-risk at 24m, continuous ADOS calibrated severity, or trajectory cluster membership?
questions.summary: Pre-register the primary target. Candidate: binary high-risk status at 24 months from ADOS-2 + clinician confirmation; continuous CSS as secondary. Trajectory clusters are exploratory.
questions.implementation: `config/model_config.yml` sets the primary target; ML pipelines read this single source. Any change triggers pipeline replay.

questions.question: How do we handle label leakage when a visit used to compute features overlaps the outcome assessment?
questions.summary: Enforce temporal split: features from visits ≤ 12 months feed a target at 24 months. Any feature within ±30 days of outcome is dropped.
questions.implementation: `split_temporal(features_df, outcome_df, lag_days=30)` enforces the gap; unit-tested with synthetic data.

questions.question: What is the minimum effective sample size per class for RF/XGB vs. deep models (CNN+LSTM, Transformer)?
questions.summary: Tabular baselines can work with ~50/class; deep sequence models need ≥150/class and heavy regularization. Document on the ML explainer.
questions.implementation: Add per-model sample-size gate in `ml_pipeline.py`; warn + skip if the cohort falls below threshold.

questions.question: How should class imbalance (ASIB ~25%, PT ~50%, TD ~25%) be addressed — reweighting, focal loss, or time-series SMOTE?
questions.summary: Default: class-weighted loss + stratified CV. Focal loss reserved for deep models. Time-series SMOTE only on segment-level oversampling, never on subjects.
questions.implementation: Policy documented in `dashboard_schema.md`; `class_weighting_policy` key in model_config.yml.

questions.question: Which secondary targets (motor delay, language delay) give enough power for multi-task learning?
questions.summary: Estimate class prevalence and pilot effective-sample-size per secondary target. Multi-task heads share the encoder; per-target loss weighting.
questions.implementation: Run pre-analysis power simulation in `scripts/power_sim_multitask.R`; gate multi-task adoption on power ≥ 0.8.

questions.question: What is the preferred mixed-effects specification (random intercepts vs. intercepts+slopes) for HRV trajectories by group?
questions.summary: Start with random intercepts + random slopes on age_months; compare vs. intercepts-only via AIC/BIC and likelihood-ratio tests. Group × time fixed interaction is the scientific term of interest.
questions.implementation: Workflow in `src/models/mixed_effects.R` using lme4::lmer; model comparison logged to `reports/mixed_effects_selection.md`.

questions.question: For Latent Growth Curve Models, do we fit linear, quadratic, or piecewise slopes given 9 unevenly spaced timepoints?
questions.summary: HRV maturation is non-linear; piecewise (knot at 6 months) or quadratic often better. Pre-register candidate shapes; pick via CFI/RMSEA.
questions.implementation: Fit linear, quadratic, and piecewise with knots ∈ {3, 6, 12} in lavaan; compare nested models; report sensitivity.

questions.question: How are family-wise error rates controlled across the panel of HRV biomarkers?
questions.summary: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR at q=0.05 within each biomarker family (time domain, frequency domain, nonlinear). Confirmatory tests use stricter Bonferroni.
questions.implementation: Central `multiplicity.py` applies the correction by family; reports include unadjusted and adjusted p-values.

questions.question: Which covariates are pre-registered (sex, GA at birth, SES, maternal age) and which are time-varying vs. baseline?
questions.summary: Baseline fixed: sex, GA, birth weight, maternal age, SES at enrollment. Time-varying: corrected age, current SES, caregiver depression, medication.
questions.implementation: Document covariate list in `docs/statistical_analysis_plan.md`; enforce in model factory.

questions.question: What is the power analysis basis for detecting a group × time interaction at α=0.05 with 260 infants?
questions.summary: Monte Carlo simulation targeting medium interaction effect (d≈0.4) yields >0.85 power with 260 infants, 9 visits, 15% attrition. Documented in grant aim 1.
questions.implementation: `scripts/power_sim.R` reproduces the grant-year power estimate; rerun annually with observed attrition.

questions.question: How do we harmonize ECG sampling rates (1024 Hz NICU vs. 500 Hz outpatient monitors) before HRV feature extraction?
questions.summary: Resample all ECG to a common 512 Hz grid before R-peak detection to neutralize device-dependent HRV bias; validate on a paired-sample cohort.
questions.implementation: `resample_common_grid(signal, sr_in, sr_out=512)` with anti-aliasing filter; unit-tested against paired recordings.

questions.question: What is the policy for segment rejection (artifact %, ectopic beats, minimum clean length) across the cohort?
questions.summary: Accept segments with <5% ectopic, ≥5 min clean length, Malik filter variance gate. Reject others; log reason; no silent drops.
questions.implementation: `src/qc/ecg_segment_qc.py` emits per-segment `accepted` flag + reason code; dashboard aggregates rejection reasons.

questions.question: How are age-corrected z-scores computed across visits, and from which normative reference?
questions.summary: Use published NICU and term norms per biomarker; compute z-scores relative to corrected age strata in months.
questions.implementation: `normative_zscore(feature, age_months, instrument)` consults a CSV of reference stats under `data/reference/`.

questions.question: How do we align REDCap event names with NDA experiment IDs and assessment definitions?
questions.summary: Maintain an explicit crosswalk (`nda_crosswalk.csv`) mapping REDCap event_name → NDA experiment_id; unit-test both sides on every sync.
questions.implementation: CI check compares REDCap export to the crosswalk and fails on any unmapped event.

questions.question: What crosswalk resolves caregiver-report instruments (PSI, CES-D, EPDS) between English and Spanish versions?
questions.summary: Document published translations and cross-language measurement invariance checks. If invariance fails, stratify analyses by language.
questions.implementation: Measurement-invariance tests via `lavaan::cfa(group="language")`; results logged to `reports/language_invariance.md`.

questions.question: Which NDA GUIDs are required for each participant, and at what point in the protocol are they generated?
questions.summary: GUIDs generated at enrollment using the NDA GUID tool; stored in REDCap `nda_guid` field (flagged PHI); never exposed to the dashboard.
questions.implementation: `scripts/generate_nda_guid.py` wraps the NDA tool; REDCap validation rule requires the field before enrollment is marked complete.

questions.question: What is the mapping between our local variables and the NDA ndar_subject01 / bayley_scales01 / asd_screening01 data structures?
questions.summary: Maintain a 1:1 field-level map with types, allowed values, and value transforms. Exporter validates each field before submission.
questions.implementation: `data/nda/field_map.yml` is the source of truth; `scripts/nda_export.py` builds NDA-ready CSVs from it.

questions.question: How do we stage raw ECG waveforms for the NDA raw-data upload while keeping PHI stripped?
questions.summary: Re-encode EDF files with a deterministic de-identified header; store under `nda_stage/raw_ecg/<guid>/` keyed only by GUID.
questions.implementation: `scripts/deidentify_edf.py` rewrites header metadata; hash validates integrity.

questions.question: What are the NDA submission deadlines relative to R01 grant year, and what is our QA gate?
questions.summary: Submissions due 6 months after the end of each grant year. QA gate: NDA validator must pass with zero hard errors; soft warnings documented.
questions.implementation: Add scheduled task for Aug 1 / Feb 1 reminders; QA gate enforced in CI for the NDA exporter.

questions.question: How do we version NDA submissions so that corrections are traceable back to REDCap audit entries?
questions.summary: Each submission keeps a manifest (`nda_manifest_<date>.json`) capturing git SHA, REDCap audit range, and file hashes. Corrections ship as delta submissions.
questions.implementation: `scripts/nda_manifest.py` writes the manifest; delta submissions reference prior manifest IDs.

questions.question: What is the CI pipeline that re-runs the full dashboard build on every commit to catch schema drift?
questions.summary: GitHub Actions: run synthetic dashboard build + contract test + extract_context.py --check on every push. Fail on any drift.
questions.implementation: `.github/workflows/dashboard-ci.yml` wires synthetic run + contract test + drift check. Artifact retained for 14 days.

questions.question: How are random seeds controlled across Python (numpy, sklearn, torch) and R (set.seed, future.apply) for full replay?
questions.summary: Single `SEED=20260401` in `config/seeds.yml` consumed by all entry points. Torch uses `torch.manual_seed` + `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)`.
questions.implementation: `src/utils/seed.py` + `src/utils/seed.R` both read the same YAML; every script calls the helper at startup.

questions.question: What is the containerization story (docker compose) such that a collaborator can reproduce a figure from the paper in <30 minutes?
questions.summary: `docker compose up --build dashboard` runs the live runtime with pinned versions; a synthetic replay path reproduces every figure without PHI access.
questions.implementation: Pin Python 3.11, R 4.3, Chart.js 4.5.1. Provide `make replay-figure FIG=<id>` target.

questions.question: How are intermediate artifacts (feature_matrix.parquet, model weights, SHAP values) versioned — DVC, git-lfs, or MLflow?
questions.summary: DVC on a S3-compatible backend inside USC secure storage for parquet + weights; MLflow for model/SHAP metadata. No raw PHI in DVC.
questions.implementation: Bootstrap DVC in `setup_artifact_store.sh`; MLflow tracking server tied to Aim 1 experiment.

questions.question: How do we issue a manuscript-ready reproducibility appendix mapping every table and figure to a commit SHA and data hash?
questions.summary: Each figure/table is generated by a numbered Rmd or notebook that stamps the commit SHA + dashboard_data.json hash into the artifact caption.
questions.implementation: `reports/appendix_builder.Rmd` composes the appendix from per-figure YAML metadata; PDF exports via `rmarkdown::render`.
